tensions.text: The node's derivation assumes consistent top-3 identity (Schneider, Vertiv, Eaton) across sources, but the Credence Research broader-PDU-market source ranks Eaton, Schneider, ABB, Siemens, Server Technology, Raritan and HPE as top players and mentions Vertiv only via its Geist brand as having 'strong presence' — not confirmed as a top-3 leader.

Two marketintelo report variants give conflicting single/double-leader framings ('Schneider Electric and Eaton Corporation led' vs 'Schneider Electric SE led'), which is inconsistent with the node's implicit assumption that leadership among the top-3 (Schneider, Vertiv, Eaton) is stable and well-established across sources.

The node conflates the rack-PDU-specific 'Medium' concentration label (Mordor Intelligence) with the broader total-PDU-market 'Semi-Consolidated' label (P&S Intelligence) as if they describe the same market, when they are different market scopes.

absences: No search result provides a numeric CR3 (combined top-3 share) for the rack-PDU market; the only hard percentage found is the single 15.8% figure for the #1 vendor (APC/Schneider).
No numeric market share was found for the #2 or #3 ranked rack-PDU vendor (Vertiv, Eaton), leaving the extrapolation from a single data point unverified.

The Omdia Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis 2025, which explicitly states it 'includes forecast and vendor shares' using 2024 as base year, is paywalled/subscription-only and its actual vendor-share figures were not retrievable in these results.
No HHI or other explicit concentration index was found in any source; all concentration descriptions are qualitative labels ('Medium', 'Semi-Consolidated') rather than computed ratios.

Vendor lists vary in composition across sources (some add nVent, Siemens, Tripp Lite, Server Technology, Raritan, HPE), so no single authoritative 'top 3' vendor set with matching market-share percentages was found.
